name: Tim Kaine
subject: On Director Comey's firing
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

I've been alarmed by many of the Trump administration's actions over the past few months. **But President Trump 's firing of FBI Director James Comey is like nothing we've seen before.**

Director Comey was charged with leading the Justice Department's investigation into possible collusion between members of the Trump campaign or administration and a foreign government, Russia. **The decision to fire Director Comey shows how frightened the Administration is of that investigation.**

What we have here is a disturbing pattern by the White House to cover up the truth. First Acting Attorney General Sally Yates warned the Trump administration that National Security Advisor Michael Flynn had lied about his Russia contacts and was open to blackmail. The president had Yates fired, yet he kept Flynn on for 18 more days.

Then we learned that Attorney General Jeff Sessions had undisclosed meetings with the Russians, and he announced that he would recuse himself from the Justice Department's Russia investigation. Now we learn that, despite saying he would recuse himself, A.G. Sessions was personally involved in the decision to fire Director Comey. These actions are strongly reminiscent of the Nixon era.

**It 's clear to me that the only way we'll get to the bottom of this is with an independent special prosecutor, one whose mandate is not directed by the Attorney General or anyone else allied with the Trump administration.**

**[Derek, will you join me in calling for a special prosecutor by adding your name immediately?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=wu%2B9kgW2VML5u0TjBE5HrvJZMIwNUnK8)**

Thank you,
Tim

name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: Election Reminder: Virginians Have 4 Days Left
body:
| | As a Christian, we MUST vote, even in the primaries!
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=170511_va_reg&n=1&v=8)
| Dear Peter,
My Faith Votes is dedicated to equipping you to act on your faith through one of the most sacred rights we have as Americans - choosing our leaders with our vote. Just as Nehemiah's men rebuilt their city with a sword in one hand and a trowel in the other, we too must stand guard for our country to both defend and rebuild it, and that can begin in the voting booth. Our records indicate that you live in the state of Virginia, and therefore, you have a registration deadline coming up **IN JUST A FEW DAYS**. To vote in the upcoming primary, which includes voting for Governor, **you must be registered by May 15th**. If you are unsure if you are registered, [take 30 seconds to check here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fthink%2Ffor-voters%2Fcheck-your-status%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=170511_va_reg&n=2&v=8). If you discover you need to register, you can [register directly through the State of Virginia here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fvote.elections.virginia.gov%2FRegistration%2FEligibility&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=170511_va_reg&n=3&v=8).

**Share this email with other Virginians** and encourage them to join you as you pray, think and vote. Faith makes America stronger, Jason Yates
Chief Executive Officer

name: signature-needed@dscc.org
subject: Jeff Sessions & James Comey
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Jeff Sessions failed to recuse himself from the FBI's investigation of the Trump administration's Russia ties.
---
[****](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e733/2b07114c/53071de7/736ed6f0/97258990/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/trump-sessions-investigation?source=PT-20170511-Signature_DS-ProsHV")
Team -- In January, Attorney General Jeff Sessions lied under oath during his Congressional confirmation hearings and said "I did not have communications with the Russians."

Then in March, after the _Washington Post_ discovered he didn't disclose those contacts, he recused himself from any investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election or into ties between the Donald Trump campaign and Russian officials.

**But this week, Sessions was deeply involved in Trump 's firing of FBI Director James Comey after the FBI escalated its probe into the administration's connections to Russia.

[Conflicts of interests are piling up that we need to get to the bottom of and we can't do it without an independent investigation. Add your name to demand a special prosecutor.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e733/2b07114c/53071de7/736ed6f0/97258990/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/trump-sessions-investigation?source=PT-20170511-Signature_DS-ProsHV")**

Sessions not only was supposed to refrain from making decisions over the Russia investigation, but he then made a recommendation to remove Comey that Donald Trump accepted.

Apparently Mr. Sessions came up with the reasons to fire Comey, like how handling Hillary Clinton's emails prompted the dismissal -- despite both Trump and Sessions praising Comey for those actions in the final days of the presidential campaign.

**It doesn 't add up and we need to find out why Sessions betrayed his pledge of recusal.

Add your name right now to find out the truth with an independent investigation:

[http://dscc.org/demand-special-prosecutor](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e733/2b07114c/53071de7/736ed6f0/97258990/VEsF/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/trump-sessions-investigation?source=PT-20170511-Signature_DS-ProsHV")**

| | Peter, I have to be honest -- I'm still in a bit of shock that House Republicans passed a bill that would rob 24 million Americans of their health insurance.

**In the health care survey my team sent you last month, over 90% of you told me that you thought Republicans would introduce an alternative to their American Health Care Act (AHCA) after it failed.** Well, last week they went further -- they passed an even more damaging version of the same bill and then marched over to the White House to celebrate.

Now it's time for me and my colleagues in the Senate to do everything we can to stop this thing. **[Join me and several other Democratic senators to tell the GOP to stop trying to gut our health care.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c267f/7377f838/3605a131/51fb515c/2615679146/VEsE/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/tellthegop/?code=donnelly&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fjd-ahcaja%3Frefcode%3D20170511%26amount%3D15")**

I've been working with my colleagues for years to improve the Affordable Care Act, not repeal it. I was proud to have voted for the ACA in 2010. It provides a framework to allow more than 400,000 Hoosiers access to health care through HIP 2.0 and ensures that the millions of Hoosiers with pre-existing conditions can afford the care they need.

The Republicans' AHCA would erase all that progress. This bill would devastate some of our most vulnerable populations. It would raise premiums for seniors. It would restrict access to opioid and heroin treatment programs. It would even sharply cut Medicaid funds for special education programs.

Another thing you told my team in last month's survey was that you overwhelmingly approved of my stance against the GOP health care bill the first time around. **Well, you can bet I 'm going to work even harder now to fight against this version in the Senate. I will do everything I can to protect Hoosiers' access to affordable health care.** **[Join me and my colleagues to tell the GOP: Don't gut our health care.](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c267f/7377f838/3605a131/51fb515c/2615679146/VEsF/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/tellthegop/?code=donnelly&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fjd-ahcaja%3Frefcode%3D20170511%26amount%3D15")**

Let's fight this together.

Joe **** **** **** **** | [**CONTRIBUTE**](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c267f/7377f838/3605a131/51fb515f/2615679146/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/jd-donate?refcode=20170511db&amount=25")

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Building a case for obstruction of justice
body:
President Trump's firing of Comey shows that he believes he is above the people and the law. | [](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46c0/1c469400/2360d82/3549a23/1143108483/VEsH/ "https://act.legitaction.com/sign/demand-a-special-prosecutor_LP?source=20170511_r4w_sp_dt&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=20170511_r4w_sp_dt") Friend, President Trump walks and talks like an authoritarian. His firing of FBI Director Comey is further proof that he believes he is above the people and the law, and need not answer to either. He wants to be untouchable. He behaves with impunity and acts as if legal standards like obstruction of justice don't apply to him. **And without an independent investigation, Trump will most likely be proven right. He will be above the law.** A special prosecutor is the only way to restore legitimacy to the investigation into President Trump's and his administration's ties to Russian intelligence operations in the 2016 election. [_**Tell Deputy Attorney General Rod Rosenstein to fulfill his oath and defend the constitution. Appoint a special prosecutor to conduct an independent investigation of President Trump 's Russia ties.**_](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46c0/1c469400/2360d82/3549a20/1143108483/VEsE/ "https://act.legitaction.com/sign/demand-a-special-prosecutor_LP?source=20170511_r4w_sp_dt&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=2+-+Tell+Deputy+Attorney+General+Rod+Rosenst&utm_campaign=20170511_r4w_sp_dt") I am very pleased to say that many members of LegitAction see exactly what's going on here. After I asked for people's thoughts the other night, we got a lot of responses like these:
* A supporter named Jane commented on Facebook, "This is a dangerous moment for democracy and the rule of law -- as well as public trust in the legitimacy of the federal government."
* A supporter named Charlene wrote, "We need an independent prosecutor to look into the connection between Russia and Donald Trump."
* And a supporter named Candace wrote, "We need to step in."

The case for obstruction of justice by the Trump administration is being built right now. We must step in to demand that Republicans and Democrats join together so that this grave danger to our democracy is called out for what it is and remedied -- and not swept under the rug. [**_Help call out this grave danger to our democracy. Step in to support a special prosecutor and an independent investigation of President Trump now._**](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46c0/1c469400/2360d82/3549a21/1143108483/VEsF/ "https://act.legitaction.com/sign/demand-a-special-prosecutor_LP?source=20170511_r4w_sp_dt&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=3+-+Help+call+out+this+grave+danger+to+our+d&utm_campaign=20170511_r4w_sp_dt") We also must not lose sight of the larger fight at hand. Tuesday's events stem directly from our own illegitimate electoral system, which denied the will of the majority of voters and produced an illegitimate president in Donald Trump. Democratic legitimacy comes from us, the voters, and it is up to us to fight to restore it. Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy, Russ Feingold | [SIGN ON](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46c0/1c469400/2360d82/3549a5e/1143108483/VEsC/ "https://act.legitaction.com/sign/demand-a-special-prosecutor_LP?source=20170511_r4w_sp_dt&utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=4+-+SIGN+ON&utm_campaign=20170511_r4w_sp_dt")
---

If you would like to change your email or salutation, [click here](http://welcome.legitaction.com/page/m/66cf46c0/1c469400/2360d82/3549a5f/1143108483/VEsD/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/changeinfo?utm_medium=email&utm_source=feingold&utm_content=5+-+click+here&utm_campaign=20170511_r4w_sp_dt&source=20170511_r4w_sp_dt"). name: Maggie Hassan
subject: We need an independent investigation (Add your name)
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello --
President Trump's abrupt decision to fire FBI Director James Comey raises serious questions for me, as it does for many Americans.
The American people deserve a thorough explanation of how this decision came about, and we must demand details about whether the decision was influenced by the ongoing investigation into the Trump campaign and Russian interference in last year's election.
The White House's actions around Comey raise many serious questions, and the President's stated rationale doesn't make sense. That's why I believe that we need an independent special prosecutor to investigate the Trump administration's potential ties to Russia.
[**Please, join me and add your name to call for an independent prosecutor to investigate the Trump administration's potential ties with Russia.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd41/2a380d31/154b0220/fef35e/915414863/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/independentprosecutor/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
It's simple -- every single American deserves to have confidence in our democratic institutions. With so many unanswered questions, we must move forward with a Congressional investigation into Russia's interference and possible connections to the Trump campaign.
[**Add your name if you agree.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdd41/2a380d31/154b0220/fef35e/915414863/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/independentprosecutor/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for everything you do.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan

| **In Case You Missed It:**

**"Inertia vs $26M"**

**by Emerson Lynn
St. Albans Messenger
May 11, 2017**
To understand the debilitating power of inertia, and how it subtracts from the ability to solve problems, look no further than the opposition’s argument against Gov. Phil Scott’s proposal to have the state bargain with our teachers over their health care benefits.

It’s a parade of denial.

• The $26 million in savings is not real because we have already negotiated several contracts in which the 80-20 ratio is not adhered to.

• There is no guarantee that future contracts will not give back some of the gains.

• There is no guarantee that the savings will be used to reduce property taxes.

• There has been no independent verification that the governor’s proposed savings are accurate, thus there is no reason to push forward.

Additionally, the union, through its many voices, contends that the Vermont Education Health Care Initiative [VEHI] has been swallowed by the school board association and therefore no longer represents the teachers’ best interests.
Further, the “savings” touted by the governor are no more than a tax on teachers and that it’s deeply unfair that property tax relief is something they should shoulder by their lonesomes. Teachers are taxpayers, too. Any relief should be paid for by someone else.

And, finally, there is inertia itself. It’s always been done this way, and it should continue to always being done this way.

The union is the union.

As far as the contention that the savings are fiction, that can be as true as we want it to be. Contracts have already been negotiated that don’t produce the governor’s savings goal. Future contracts can also be negotiated that don’t produce the savings, and that’s as true under the present arrangement as it would be if the teachers were negotiating with the state.

It’s also true that the savings might not provide any relief for the taxpayers.

All of the opposition’s claims could prove to be true, and they most certainly would be if we continue to do what we have always done.

The difference between the governor’s plan and the opposition is that with his we have a chance, with theirs, we don’t.

If from this point forward we don’t have parity – all teachers getting the same health care package – then there is no honest expectation that the savings can be realized district to district.

We can argue about whether teachers will actually have to pay more out of pocket expenses than they do now, but the governor has made it clear that almost $50 million of the $75 million in savings would be devoted to paying for those out-of-pocket expenses. Is this something that the opposition can guarantee through individual
negotiations school board to school board?

Not a chance. Reportedly, one of the school boards has already negotiated with its teachers and has agreed to pick up all the teachers’ out of pocket costs. How is that avoided if we continue to do what we have always done?

It isn’t.

A school budget is primarily labor – roughly 80 percent. Of that amount, health care is a significant driver of costs. If the health care contract is negotiated statewide we have the chance to save the $26 million. If we don’t, we don’t. It’s just that simple.

As regards the “burden” on teachers, is it really? They would pay less in premiums, something that doesn’t happen to the average Vermonter, and the $50 million is used to defray their out-of-pocket expenses through health care savings accounts. The teachers are being asked to think carefully before going to the emergency room for care. They are being asked to pick a generic drug versus a more expensive brand name. And don’t they get the same property tax relief the rest of us would get?

If, as a system, we are paying more today to educate 25,000 fewer students, then what is the VT NEA’s answer to this unsustainable trend? If we have a student-teacher ratio that is half the national average, and if we continue to lose 1,000 students a year, then how do we adjust?

Clearly, the VT NEA’s answer is to keep doing exactly what we’ve always done.

Talk about denial.

Why would the opposition – in particular the Democratic leadership – want to act ostrich-like, refusing to acknowledge the topography affecting education in Vermont? Why would they want to be seen as slaves to a narrow special interest group – the union leadership more than the teachers themselves – and be cast as immovable when it comes to creative thought about the most efficient way to support our educational needs?

And if the teachers could bring a bit of relief to property tax payers and not be hurt in the process, why isn’t that something to be proud of ? If it’s the taxpayers who give their blessings to a school’s proposed budget, and if two of every three homes are without school-age children, then would it not be politically astute to be seen as supportive of something that makes education more affordable, and something that may offer more resources to early education and tuition help for those going on to college?

It would be, and the Democrats should impress upon the leadership the need to be creative in figuring out a way to meet the governor’s goal of providing all our teachers with the same health care benefits package. They should also explain to the union that there is power in collaboration and compromise. If they continue to play a zero-sum game, they lose, and in a sense, so do we all.

body:
Hey Derek!

Next Thursday is the Party's first fundraising dinner of the
year. We have a fun-filled evening prepared and I am looking
forward to see you as we begin to build our operation for 2018.

I am excited to hear from our special guest, Kellyanne Conway.
She happened to be in New Hampshire just yesterday discussing the
opioid crisis with HHS Sec. Tom Price and Gov. Sununu.

The Trump Administration is invested in New Hampshire's future.
Your ticket to this dinner invests in our future too.

To add to the fun, we have secured some amazing items to be
raffled and auctioned off. The items range from a private
training for two at Sig Suer Academy to a stay at the Von Trapp
Lodge on Vermont AND a chance to win NCW Juliana Bergeron's
famous lobster bisque!

Join me, because this is a night you cannot miss!

Cocktails at 5:30PM
Dinner begins promptly at 6:30PM

clean_body: Hey Derek! Next Thursday is the Party's first fundraising dinner of the year. We have a fun-filled evening prepared and I am looking forward to see you as we begin to build our operation for 2018. I am excited to hear from our special guest, Kellyanne Conway. She happened to be in New Hampshire just yesterday discussing the opioid crisis with HHS Sec. Tom Price and Gov. Sununu. The Trump Administration is invested in New Hampshire's future. Your ticket to this dinner invests in our future too. To add to the fun, we have secured some amazing items to be raffled and auctioned off. The items range from a private training for two at Sig Suer Academy to a stay at the Von Trapp Lodge on Vermont AND a chance to win NCW Juliana Bergeron's famous lobster bisque! Join me, because this is a night you cannot miss! Cocktails at 5:30PM Dinner begins promptly at 6:30PM CLICK HERE FOR TICKETS! body:
Sign the petition >>
| |

| |

| Friend,

No more waiting, no more delays, no more obstruction by Republicans.

**Our country NEEDS a special and independent counsel with full subpoena power to investigate the Trump Administration's ties to Russia.**

Donald Trump just fired the man in charge of this investigation... on the recommendation of an Attorney General who claimed to have recused himself. And now the administration is struggling to justify why.

Well, I'll tell you why. _This is a blatant power grab and possibly a cover-up._

We won't let Trump get away with this. [_**I'm joining with my colleagues in Congress to call for an independent special counsel to take over this investigation. Join us >>**_](http://fl.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e9e/622b7b5b/2d54f43/307bcc21/3857538242/VEsH/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/independentprosecutor/?code=DWS&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fdwse170510ja%3FRecurring%3Dtrue")

Last night was the last of many, many straws. No more excuses. It's time to get to the bottom of things and find out the truth.

[_**Sign our petition: demand a special counsel.**_](http://fl.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113e9e/622b7b5b/2d54f43/307bcc21/3857538242/VEsE/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/independentprosecutor/?code=DWS&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fcontribute%2Fpage%2Fdwse170510ja%3FRecurring%3Dtrue")

Thank you,

Debbie |

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Help elect this progressive to Congress
body:
We know that we can surprise the pundit class and push Rob over the top on May 25th, but he's going to need our support.
#
Friends,
Rob Quist and his grassroots team of supporters in Montana are working harder than ever to win Montana's special election to Congress. Coming from a rural state like I do, Rob is raising issues that resonate with people in a way that the Beltway establishment just hasn't figured out. We know that we can surprise the pundit class and push Rob over the top on May 25th, but he's going to need our support.
**[Republican Super PACs have been flooding Montana airwaves with negative attacks. Rob is working to share his progressive message and win the special election on May 25th -- help him out by contributing $27 to his campaign for Congress today.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d0c/43f17890/dbc79e03/6203a947/904404120/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/or-quist-mfa?refcode=em170511-full")**
On every major issue, Rob Quist is leading the way for Montana. He is a devoted supporter of Medicare for All, Planned Parenthood, raising the minimum wage to a living wage, and equal pay for equal work. He also wants to get big money out of politics by overturning Citizens United. I intend to be campaigning with Rob next weekend all over Montana. Together, we can give Rob an enormous boost in the final stretch of his campaign and send a message to Washington that progressives across the country are doing more than just mounting challenges -- they're running to win.
This is especially important in Montana, where President Trump's executive order to review the status of our national parks puts natural treasures like Glacier and Yellowstone in the crosshairs of Wall Street and the fossil fuel industry. Rob believes, like I do, that public lands should be passed down to the next generation, not sold off to the highest bidder. After the incredible turnout at the People's Climate March, it's clear that the American people are on Rob's side.
Rob spent the last year campaigning with us during our campaign for the Democratic presidential nomination. It's time we returned the favor.
**[Please chip in $27 to Rob Quist's campaign to give him the boost he needs for Montana's May 25th special election and add his independent voice to the debate in Washington.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/24fb0d0c/43f17890/dbc79e03/6203a947/904404120/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/or-quist-mfa?refcode=em170511-full")**
It's in moments like these that we need to rally to the cause and advance our progressive agenda. A victory in Montana would not just be a step forward for that beautiful state, it will be a step forward for progressive politics all across the country. That's why I'm adding my voice to this fight -- I hope you will add yours too.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
